_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL state-leg batch (Senate SD-26/27 + House HD-26/27/28): lori-berman (SD-26 D Senate Minority Leader, FreedomIndex 3% progressive, PP-PAC endorsed, abortion-rights/ERA sponsor, red-flag author + 'Gun Safety Champion', NAY SB4-C; no cross-party deviation, 0), ben-albritton (SD-27 R President of FL Senate, devout publicly-professing Christian, FreedomIndex 73%; HARD YEA SB300/HB543 + SB254/HB1 party-line + championed SB4-C anti-sanctuary; CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION on 2A: YEA 2018 Parkland SB7026 red-flag/age-21 + blocks lowering long-gun age 3 sessions; CURRENCY FIX: won Nov-2024, 4-yr term next up 2028 NOT 2026), nan-cobb (HD-26 R freshman, HARD YEA HB289 fetal-personhood + SB4-C, FRTL-endorsed, NRA member, Baptist, 100), richard-gentry (HD-27 R freshman, HARD YEA HB759 pro-2A long-gun-age repeal + all-3-sheriffs endorsed + anti-sanctuary campaign; DEVIATION FLAGS: pro-life WITH rape/incest exceptions + 2A-with-location-limits noted in profile, 100), bill-partington (HD-28 R freshman, ex-Ormond Beach mayor + current asst public defender; HARD YEA SB4-C + unconditional-2A platform, Presbyterian elder, 100).

set.office: State Senator (SD-26 · Senate Minority Leader; on 2026 ballot)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-26 (Palm Beach) senator since 2018 (House 2010-2018); became Senate Minority Leader Apr 24, 2025. Term ends Nov 3, 2026 — on the 2026 ballot. Freedom Index lifetime 3% (one of the chamber's lowest liberty scores). Scored from HARD bill sponsorships, group endorsements/ratings, and floor statements; a few 2023 party-line bans (SB300/HB543) were not individually pulled and are left null rather than inferred.

lori-berman@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Solidly progressive Palm Beach Democrat; NO cross-party deviation found. Sanctity: pro-choice, Florida Planned Parenthood PAC-endorsed (2022), drafted a bill to repeal the 15-week ban AND enshrine a statutory abortion right, called for repeal of the 6-week ban after Amendment 4 failed. Marriage/sexuality: opposed SB254 (minor gender-care ban) and HB1557 ('Don't Say Gay'); filed an ERA-ratification bill. Family: opposed HB1 universal vouchers ('corporate welfare for parents already paying for private schools'). Guns: original sponsor of FL's Risk Protection Order (red-flag) law (in 2018 SB7026), still pushing to expand it; named a 'Gun Safety Champion' by Ban Assault Weapons Now; advocates 'assault weapon'/high-cap-magazine bans; filed safe-storage SB80 (2026). Immigration: voted NO on SB4-C (2025 'TRUMP Act' anti-sanctuary). Justice: voted NO on SB1804 (death penalty for child-sex-traffickers) — not scored as a clean cell (mixed against her DV-victim-protection bills). Christian-liberty cells null (Jewish; no policy votes located).

0.note: Pro-choice; drafted bill to enshrine a statutory abortion right and repeal bans — rejects personhood-from-conception

1.note: Sponsors repeal of abortion bans; opposes restrictions let alone abolition

4.note: Florida Planned Parenthood PAC-endorsed (2022) — aligned with abortion-industry PAC

2.note: Opposed SB254 minor gender-care ban; defends transgender adults/minors — rejects biological-sex policy

4.note: Opposed HB1557 'Don't Say Gay'; filed ERA bill — supports LGBTQ in schools/policy

0.note: Voted NO on HB1 (2023) universal vouchers; called it 'corporate welfare' — opposes school choice

1.note: Used a parental-rights argument against SB254 — opposite of the conservative parental-consent-to-restrict frame; no clean cell

0.note: Jewish; no public-profession-of-faith policy vote located

1.note: Pro-voting-access posture but no specific photo-ID/citizenship vote isolated

3.note: No isolated mail-in/drop-box vote (HB1205 was a petition-circulator bill, not mail-in)

0.note: No affirmative pro-police advocacy meeting the high bar

2.note: Mixed: pro-victim DV bills vs NAY on SB1804 (death penalty for child-sex-traffickers) — no clean cell
Voted NO on SB4-C (2025) anti-sanctuary 'TRUMP Act'; filed enforcement-oversight bill — opposes anti-sanctuary policy

0.note: 'Gun Safety Champion' (Ban Assault Weapons Now); uniform gun-control record — opposes constitutional carry

1.note: Original sponsor of FL red-flag (Risk Protection Order) law, pushing to expand it; advocates 'assault weapon'/magazine bans — supports gun-control restrictions

set.office: State Senator (SD-27 · FL Senate presiding officer 2024-2026; won Nov-2024, 4-yr term next up 2028)

profile.religion: Christian (lifetime member and ~20-year elder, First Christian Church of Wauchula)
profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-27 (Charlotte/DeSoto/Hardee + Lee/Polk portions) senator since 2018 (House 2010-2018); unanimously elected President of the FL Senate for 2024-2026. CURRENCY FIX: won re-election Nov 5, 2024 to a 4-year Senate term — NEXT UP 2028, NOT seeking reelection in 2026 (record previously said incumbent_seeking_reelection). Fourth-generation citrus grower. Freedom Index lifetime 73%. Scored from HARD flsenate roll calls (SB300, HB543) + party-line marquee votes he was in the majority on (SB254, HB1) + championing SB4-C + repeated public professions of Christ. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION captured on 2A.

ben-albritton@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Conservative, devout publicly-professing Christian Senate President. HARD: YEA SB300 (2023 6-week abortion ban, Senate 26-13, not a GOP defector); YEA HB543 (2023 permitless carry, Senate 27-13). Party-line marquee (in GOP majority, no defection): SB254 (minor gender-care ban), HB1 (universal vouchers). Immigration: negotiated and championed the 2025 SB4-C/SB2-C anti-sanctuary special-session laws, stood with DeSantis at signing, joint statement backing Trump enforcement (note: brokered a moderated Senate compromise version). Christian liberty: as President repeatedly professed Christ — 'I am not just a professing Christian, I am a practicing Christian and being Christ-like matters'; said officials should be 'guided by faith.' CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (2A): voted YEA on the 2018 Parkland SB7026 (raised long-gun age to 21, waiting period, created red-flag orders) and as Senate President has BLOCKED House bills to restore the long-gun age to 18 for three straight sessions — scored FALSE on self_defense q1. Softer flags noted: a 'won't change it' posture on the 6-week ban after 2024, the moderated immigration compromise, and the Everglades-criticized SB2508 (DeSantis-vetoed).

0.note: YEA SB300 (2023) 6-week heartbeat ban; campaign affirms 'the right to life for all individuals, no matter how small or young' — pro-life record

1.note: Voted for a restriction, not abolition; post-2024 'won't change it' posture — abolition bar not met

4.note: No documented abortion-industry money; not inferred

2.note: SB254 (2023 minor gender-care ban) passed party-line; in GOP majority, no defection — rejects transgender-ideology policy

0.note: HB1 (2023 universal vouchers) passed party-line as Majority Leader; supports 'parental choice' in education — pro-school-choice

3.note: HB3 porn-age-verification individual vote not isolated

4.note: Foster-care emphasis too general to score faith-based-adoption

0.note: Publicly professes Christ in office as Senate President ('a practicing Christian... being Christ-like matters'); says officials should be 'guided by faith' — affirms public profession of Christ

3.note: Opened session in personal prayer (customary) — no policy position on prayer-in-public-bodies isolated

2.note: Tax-cut/property-tax agenda but no balanced-budget-amendment position

1.note: 'Floridian citizens are the ones doing the voting' is too general; no specific photo-ID vote isolated

0.note: No affirmative pro-police advocacy meeting the high bar located
Immigration framed as cooperative federalism with Trump/WH, not 10th-Amendment nullification

2.note: Championed and passed SB4-C (2025) anti-sanctuary law; joint statement backing Trump enforcement — opposes sanctuary policy (note: brokered a moderated compromise)

3.note: SB1718 E-Verify individual vote not isolated

4.note: SB264 foreign-land individual vote not isolated

0.note: YEA HB543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry (Senate 27-13, not a defector); NRA member, CWL holder

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: YEA 2018 Parkland SB7026 (age-21, waiting period, created red-flag orders); as Senate President has blocked restoring the long-gun age to 18 for 3 sessions — supports gun-control restrictions

nan-cobb@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Conservative freshman; no cross-party deviation. HARD YEA roll calls: CS/HB289 (2026 'Civil Liability for the Wrongful Death of an Unborn Child' — fetal-personhood, House 76-34) and SB4-C (2025 immigration/anti-sanctuary special session, House 85-29). Florida Right to Life-endorsed (2024 primary + general). NRA member (affiliation only — not an A-rating/endorsement, so 2A cells left null). Baptist. Co-introducer HB127 (cursive instruction) and primary sponsor HB211 (right-to-farm) — not rubric-scoreable. Genuinely thin: NO EVIDENCE on marriage/sexuality, Christian-liberty law, economics, elections, 10th Amendment, and 2A specifics; those cells null.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: HD-27 (Lake/Marion/Volusia) freshman Republican, first elected 2024 (NOT 2022), sworn Nov 19, 2024; on the Lake County 2026 roster. IDENTITY CONFIRMED as the longtime Florida Home Builders Assn general counsel / ex-Public Counsel / Florida Housing Hall of Fame figure. Only the 2025 session exists, so 2023 bills (SB300/HB543/HB1/SB254/SB264) predate him and are null. Scored from one HARD 2025 roll call (HB759), all-district-sheriff endorsements, and cited campaign-issue statements. DEVIATIONS noted in notes.

richard-gentry@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Mainstream establishment-conservative freshman; no liberal votes found, but two documented MODERATIONS worth flagging for readers. HARD: YEA HB759 (2025, lowering long-gun purchase age 21->18 — NRA-backed repeal of the post-Parkland age restriction; House 78-34, not a GOP defector). Public justice: endorsed by EVERY sheriff in the district (Volusia/Chitwood, Lake/Grinnell, Marion/Woods) and campaigns on empowering local sheriffs — affirmative law-enforcement backing. Immigration: campaign platform 'giving local Sheriffs the power to enforce immigration laws' (anti-sanctuary). DEVIATION FLAGS (left as null cells, not scored against him, but documented): (1) pro-life WITH exceptions for life-of-mother/rape/incest — does not meet the strict personhood-from-conception bar, so sanctity q0 null; (2) supports 2A 'but thinks there should be restrictions on where you can take them, e.g., not in schools.' Methodist; Free Mason. SB4-C individual vote could NOT be verified (presumed-Yea but UNVERIFIED) — left null, not scored.

0.note: Pro-life WITH exceptions for life-of-mother/rape/incest — qualified stance does not meet personhood-from-conception bar; not scored either way

1.note: Explicitly favors exceptions — does not advocate abolition

Presbyterian church elder (47+ years). Biographical nuance (NOT scored as a position): he is a career prosecutor turned current Assistant Public Defender (felony-division chief) — defense-side day job. NO EVIDENCE on life, marriage, family, Christian-liberty law, economics, elections, or 10th Amendment — those cells null. Pre-filed 2026 bills (crypto-jurisdiction HB601, hate-crime HB1535, code-inspector body cameras) are sponsorships of unverified direction — not scored.
